Ligularia uniflora, also known as Limniris uniflora, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ligularia uniflora include sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ligularia uniflora is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Limniris uniflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. This family is commonly known as the iris family and consists of a diverse range of flowering plants. Limniris uniflora is native to the mountainous regions of Asia, where it thrives in damp and rocky environments. The exact classification of Limniris uniflora may be subject to change as new research emerges, but it is currently classified within the Iridaceae family. More specifically, it is often grouped together with other irises within the subfamily Iridoideae.
